transcript.text: Hello?
Hi, this is Justin with McGuire Capital. How are you?
I'm doing well. How about you?
Good. I'm sorry, I do not want to butcher your name. Can you say your name for me?
It's Sabha.
Sabha. Okay. I, that's what I thought, but I just wanted to make sure I didn't say it wrong. No problem. Well, how's your day been? Sabiha?
A little busy. Yeah, but it's okay.

Yeah. Good. Well, like I said, my name is Justin. I'm with McGuire Capital, and the reason I'm calling you today is, you know, uh, you had set up some time, um, to maybe talk about, um, making sure that you have some consistent income in retirement. Is that correct? Yep. Yeah. Okay. Well, I'm in here in, I'm in Oklahoma City. Where are you located at?
I'm in New York.

In New York. Great. Sorry, I'm just taking some notes here. Um, well, what I'd like to do is share a little bit about my process and, uh, what I want to do is let you get to know me a little bit. I want to get to know who you are and I want to understand, you know, more in detail about what your goals are, where you're at, and kind of go from there. Okay? So, okay. What I want, um, so the way I kind of work is, like I said, I want to try to get to know you a little bit and you get to know me. Um, so Sia, what do you do? What do you do for work?

I work in Bank of New York. Um, okay, so first of all, I just wanna understand, I know that I've probably contacted you through, um, online and some social media. I wanna understand, um, if there is any writeup that I can read about your organization, what you do, uh, what are the services, how are they charged?
Charged? Absolutely. So I can explain a little bit.
Mm-hmm <affirmative>. Okay. So first,

So first off, uh, my website is McGuire cap.com mm-hmm <affirmative>. And I can actually text that to you if you'd like, really quick. Um mm-hmm <affirmative>. And you can go in and you can see our website. I am sending that to you now. Um, and we are a registered investment advisor. Um, we currently manage about $250 million here in Oklahoma City. Um, well, but we service all 50 states. We have clients in all 50 states. Uh, we are series 65 fiduciary. So, um, what that means is by law we have to do what's best for the client. We get paid for our advice, not making trades or, or doing anything like that. Now, we do earn a commission on some other products, you know, that we do like some insurance products and annuities and stuff like that, if we were to do that. But that, that's paid by the insurance is and not by you.

Okay. The only thing that you would ever pay us for would be, uh, an advisor fee. Okay? Okay. Uh, you're not getting charged for this call you every, every time you, you don't get charged every time you talk to me or anything like that, okay? Mm-hmm <affirmative>. Um, I really just, and those are great questions and I want to answer all of your questions, but, um, what we are, we're an independent RIA, like I said, registered investment advisory, and, um, we do virtually every type of investment there are. We do stock market, we do annuities, we do, uh, life insurance, we do Medicare, we do literally just about everything. And because we're an independent, we, we manage assets a little differently. You know, a lot of the big box firms like, uh, Merrill Lynch or Chase or, uh, JP Morgan, you know, a lot of those bigger firms, they will get a risk assessment for from you and then stick you in a model, uh, of mutual funds based on your risk tolerance.

Mm-hmm <affirmative>. Here we are more, uh, active asset managers, meaning, yes, we still use some mutual funds, but we use 'em very limited. Um, we tailor individual retirement plans to the indi, you know, to you what, what that means is we'll pick, pick a group of stocks, um, individual equities, um, to invest for you based on your risk tolerance. And it's, and it's a fluid portfolio, meaning, you know, we'll, we'll make adjustments to that as we go along to try to get the best return possible. Now, because we do that, we don't average, you know, in line with the s and p 500, we have beat the s and p 500 year over year because of the way we do that. And last year our firm average was 41%. Um, now, I can't promise you 41%, you know, uh, and I can't guarantee anything, but, you know, last year the s and p did about 24 and we did 41. Um, and we do that, like I said, by actively managing the portfolios and not, you know, we're, we're very active and not passive in that sense. Does that make sense?
Mm-hmm <affirmative>. Yep.

Yeah. And, um, our average fee is between one and 2% per year. Um, and that's typically tiered based off of amount in your portfolio. Um, and I know that sounds kind of expensive on the 2% side, but what I like to tell people is, you know, you can spend, you can pay 1% and get 10, or you can pay 2% and get 20, so you know which one would you rather have. Does that make sense?
Yep. Mm-hmm
<affirmative>. Yeah. So, um, does that answer kind of answer your questions a little bit?

Yes, it does. So my portfolio is managed by Voya. Okay. And then I also have Fidelity, but Voya is like the primary provider for my organization, for my company, okay. So they manage the portfolio for now, but I really needed help. Like, so say for example, we are mean, I wouldn't say high owner, but we are on the higher bracket and we pay a lot of taxes, so I wanna figure out, and then we have college fees. So for me, this really is, um, one, how do we, you know, strategy, what is the strategy for us to save tax, save on taxes, number one, and number two, how can we efficiently pay for colleges so that, you know, our retirement is not jeopardized. And of course, the third is to secure, uh, retirement as soon as possible so that we can retire early, hopefully if there are other income sources. So that was really like the tax planning, you know, the strategy going into retirement. How do we efficiently invest now so that we can retire early?
Yeah, absolutely. Um, there are a few, quite a few different tools that we use for tax management and, um, limiting, uh, the amount of taxes we pay. Uh, are you married?
Yes.
Okay. Are you, do you both earn?
Yes.

Okay. So one of the biggest advantages or tax adv advantaged ways is to create, uh, a company, an organization, right? Mm-hmm <affirmative>. And the way we do that is we, we find something that you can do that would actually, we, an operating company, an LLC is the most common, and by owning a company, you are allowed to have a 401k, and having a 401k allows you much larger tax advantage contribution limits. So instead of paying the taxes on your income, you could contribute to the 401k, uh, and get that, that tax break. Does that make sense? So instead of paying Uncle Sam, you're paying yourself.

Yeah. Mm-hmm <affirmative>. So we do have our 4 0 1 Ks. We do have, I'm, I'm looking for more like investment properties. Where do we invest? Should we buy, should we rent? Should we, like how, where all can we save money?

Yeah, I mean, I would, I, I would love to get into all that, but you know, without knowing all the full details, I mean obviously mm-hmm <affirmative>. I wouldn't be able to give you great numbers. Um, and you know, the purpose of of this call is to just kind of get an idea of where you're at, what you want to do, and then, um, typically what I do is book a second call with you and it would be on Zoom, and then you can kind of show me, you know, what you have and where, and then I can make suggestions based on that. Does that make sense?
Okay. Yeah.

Yeah, because I mean, I can throw ideas at you all day long, but if it's not really what you're asking for, then I don't, I mean, I don't wanna waste your time. Yeah. Does that make sense? Mm-hmm <affirmative>. Certainly. But yeah, there's, there's a lots of tax strategies. We have actually have a CPA in our office. Um, so, you know, we are very tax adverse and tax aware, um, and we deal with real estate transactions, you know, we deal with, um, business owners all the time that, you know, um, that deal with, um, buying and selling property and having rental properties and all that sort of thing. Um, so I'm, we're very well versed here in this office with that sort of thing, and I would love to help you with that.
Sounds good. Mm-hmm <affirmative>.
Yeah. Um, so just to kind of get an idea, so what's your husband's name?
I-S-M-A-I-O, Ishmael.
Okay. Alright. And does, uh, what does Ishmael do?
He is in demand supply management.
Okay. Sorry, I'm just taking some notes here. And would Ishmael be on the call with you for the next call or no?
Uh, I, he's actually traveling. I don't think so.

Okay, no worries. No worries. Um, so, so yeah, um, there are, like I said, there are a lot of different, uh, tax advantage strategies that we could absolutely help you with. Um, I think, I think you would be very happy with, with some ideas that we could come up with, um mm-hmm <affirmative>. So what I would like to do is, uh, schedule another call. Um, like I said, I don't, what I'd like to do is schedule a Zoom meeting with you and give you time to kind of gather everything, like statements and that sort of thing so that you could share those with me, um, and kind of get an idea of where your assets are sitting. And, um, I need really accurate numbers so that I can give you accurate information. Does that make sense? Mm-hmm <affirmative>.
Okay.

Okay. Um, I'm not a pushy sales guy. I've just, I'm here to give you information and if you like, uh, what I have to say, then great. If not, then, you know, we just discuss it and, and move on. Um, so, okay. So you're really interested in, in taxes, how to save on taxes and strategy for paying for colleges. How many kids do you have?
We have three.
Three. Are they all getting close to college age?
So I have one who's graduated and two are in college.
Okay. All right. I, I have three also one graduated, one graduating this year. Mm-hmm <affirmative>. And one that's a freshman in high school learning how to drive. That's not fun.
Nice. So I think we have about the same age
<laugh>. Yeah, sounds like it. I
Have a junior, I have a freshman, junior and one just graduated last year.
Uh, boys, girls.
I have all three girls.
I have all three girls.
<laugh>.

Well, I'm, I'm in a house full of, I'm in a house full of girls. Actually. I, I quite love having, being a girl dad though. I don't think that, I think I really actually love it. I, uh, my girls and I are very close.
That's nice. It's very nice.
Uh, okay, so if you don't mind, do you mind setting up a, a time to, to get on, jump on a Zoom meeting?
Um, so actually it's a big holiday this weekend. Um, I think next week sounds better. Maybe like a Friday.
Yeah. That's great. Let me look here at my calendar. Uh, uh, I'm actually booked up, um, next Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday. I do have some time on Monday and Tuesday of next week.
Um, actually I work in the city, so Monday, Tuesday sounds a little okay, but weekdays I'm like Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, I'm in the office. Fridays I work from home, so.
Okay.
Um, so then the third,
Yeah, I have some time on the third.
Okay. I can put you down.
Okay. I will send you a, a zoom invite. Okay.
Okay.
April the third, is there a special time that you would like? I'm pretty open that day right now. I have,
Yeah,
I have four
O'clock better up

Four o'clock. Yeah, I can do that. 4:00 PM Eastern time. Okay. So B is very, very lovely meeting you. Um, like I said, some homework for you, if you don't mind, uh, get mm-hmm <affirmative>. Get your statements together and so you could share those with me and, um, we'll have a chat more in depth about exactly what you're looking for and, um, and I'll have, um, some strategies for you Okay. About, you know, saving on taxes and paying for college.
Okay. Perfect. Thank you so much.
You so much. I'll talk to you on the third.
Yep. Bye.
All right, bye-bye.

review.summary: Justin, you built strong rapport by using the prospect's name and finding common ground about your families, which created a comfortable atmosphere. However, you spent too much time delivering a generic pitch about your firm's performance and fees instead of asking open-ended questions to deeply explore her specific needs around tax planning and college funding. To improve, focus on listening more and asking follow-up questions about her current assets, income, and timeline before offering solutions, and directly tie your value to her expressed concerns with concrete examples.

categories.name: Rapport building: assess whether the advisor opened with a w
categories.feedback: You did a great job building rapport by using the prospect's name naturally, showing genuine interest in her situation, and finding common ground about having three daughters. To reach a 9-10, try to slow down and listen more before jumping into your pitch—let her fully express her needs first, and mirror her energy more closely (she seemed a bit reserved, so match that with a calmer tone).

categories.name: Needs discovery: assess whether the advisor asked open-ended
categories.feedback: The advisor spent most of the call pitching their firm's services and performance rather than asking open-ended questions to uncover the prospect's current situation, goals, or fears. While the prospect shared some needs (tax planning, college funding), the advisor did not explore these in depth or ask follow-up questions to deepen understanding. To improve, focus on listening more and asking questions about the prospect's specific assets, income, timeline, and pain points before offering solutions.

categories.name: Solution presentation: assess whether the advisor tied the s
categories.feedback: The advisor did not present a tailored solution tied to the prospect's specific needs (tax planning, college funding, early retirement). Instead, he gave a generic overview of his firm's approach and fees, and only briefly mentioned tax strategies without concrete examples or clear value for Sabha's situation. To improve, directly connect your solution to her expressed concerns, use plain language with specific examples, and avoid lengthy explanations of your process before addressing her goals.

categories.name: Objection handling: assess whether the advisor acknowledged
categories.feedback: You acknowledged the client's questions about fees and services directly, but you could have asked more clarifying questions to understand the root of her concerns before diving into explanations. You maintained confidence and turned her interest in tax planning into an opportunity to schedule a follow-up, but you missed a chance to proactively surface common concerns like fees or performance guarantees earlier in the conversation.

criteria.description: Rapport building: assess whether the advisor opened with a warm personalized greeting, used the prospect's name naturally, showed genuine interest in the prospect's situation before moving to business, mirrored the prospect's tone and energy, created moments of authentic connection, and listened actively before responding.
criteria.success_condition: The advisor created a genuinely comfortable and trusting atmosphere. The prospect opened up freely, there were multiple natural moments of connection, and the advisor consistently listened and acknowledged before responding. Score 9–10 for exceptional warmth; 1–2 for cold or dismissive tone with no attempt to connect.

criteria.description: Needs discovery: assess whether the advisor asked open-ended questions, uncovered the prospect's current financial situation (assets, accounts, income), identified the prospect's goals and desired outcomes, uncovered fears and pain points, explored timeline and urgency, listened more than they talked, and asked follow-up questions that deepened understanding.
criteria.success_condition: The advisor comprehensively uncovered the prospect's current situation, goals, fears, and timeline. The prospect felt heard. The advisor asked more questions than they made statements and dug deeper on key concerns. Score 9–10 for comprehensive discovery; 1–2 for launching into a pitch with no discovery.

criteria.description: Solution presentation: assess whether the advisor tied the solution directly to the needs the prospect expressed, explained it in plain language, used concrete examples or case studies, clearly articulated the value proposition for this specific prospect, avoided unexplained jargon, presented with confidence, and kept the presentation appropriately concise.
criteria.success_condition: The solution was clearly connected to the prospect's specific situation, compelling, and easy to understand. The advisor used concrete examples and tied everything back to what the prospect said they cared about. Score 9–10 for highly tailored and clear presentation; 1–2 for no real solution presented or an irrelevant one.

criteria.description: Objection handling: assess whether the advisor acknowledged objections before responding, asked clarifying questions to understand the root of the objection, responded with empathy and without defensiveness, provided specific relevant answers, proactively surfaced common concerns before they arose, turned objections into opportunities to reinforce value, and maintained confidence after handling an objection.
criteria.success_condition: All objections were handled with empathy, specificity, and confidence. The advisor proactively surfaced and addressed common concerns before they became objections, and turned each objection into an opportunity to reinforce value. Score 9–10 for masterful handling; 1–2 for ignored or trust-damaging responses.
